title: DOS via Mutation Aliasing in GraphQL Account Recovery Phone Number Verification API

profile.name: HackerOne
profile.about: Vulnerability disclosure should be safe, transparent, and rewarding.

summaries.content:
# Important Guideline Update Regarding DoS Vulnerabilities

This report was submitted and resolved in accordance with our previous program guidelines. As of October 2025, HackerOne has updated its policy regarding Denial-of-Service (DoS) vulnerabilities to reflect more stringent safety and testing requirements.

We appreciate the researcher’s thorough documentation of the GraphQL mutation aliasing issue and awarded a bounty consistent with the guidelines in effect at the time of submission. Under our current guidelines, similar DoS reports will be handled in accordance with the updated guidelines, which can be reviewed here: [link].

Key Updates
- DoS testing is now subject to stricter safeguards, including defined testing windows
- Single-request, single-user, single-IP testing constraints are more rigorously enforced
- The reward framework for DoS findings has been refined to align with these requirements

Researchers are encouraged to review the updated guidelines before conducting any DoS-related testing and to focus on vulnerability classes that align with current program priorities.

This disclosure recognizes the researcher’s contribution under our prior guidelines while transparently communicating our updated approach to DoS vulnerability assessment.
